The J6 & Cyber Defense Directorate in the Israel Defense Forces is responsible for cyber defense, communication, wireless transmission, computerization, and control over intelligence information in the IDF. 

The Cyber Defense Directorate provides commanders in the field with the appropriate technology needed to manage a situation in the best possible way. The Directorate's main objectives are to initiate, develop, and strengthen the IDF's technological systems. With growing technology, the Directorate has become a center and leader in combating cyber threats towards Israel.

companyResolutionResult.description: Verbit delivers AI transcription and captioning products to the global brands who depend on speed, accuracy, and insights. Verbit's Captivate technology and expert human transcribers are purpose-built for “speech intensive industries”, delivering unmatched results in the classroom, the boardroom, and the world’s most-watched events. And products like Legal Visor offer in-the-moment insights and suggestions during legal proceedings. Whether you're a content creator expanding your audience, an educator improving student outcomes, or a litigator seeking a competitive edge, our comprehensive verbal intelligence platform transforms how organizations drive value from speech.

currentPositions.description:
Serving as the primary security authority for Verbit, bridging the gap between technical engineering, business strategy, and regulatory compliance.

- Security Architecture & Design: Leading design reviews and threat modeling for new features, converting high-level requirements into actionable technical specifications for R&D and IT.

- Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC): Defining organizational risk appetite, updating policies to meet evolving regulations, and delivering executive-level security reports.

- Vendor & Budget Strategy: Managing the full Third-Party Risk Management lifecycle - from technical assessment and risk definition to budget planning and contract negotiation.

- Business Enablement: Accelerating business growth by translating client security requirements into product capabilities and managing sales-support activities.

- Validation & Oversight: Orchestrating the scope and execution of penetration testing and security validation activities across products and global infrastructure.
currentPositions.title: Security Architect

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
צוות בית הספר יקדם את כלל הבאים בשעריו
להגיע למיטבם בתחומים הבאים:

מימוש עצמי, עושר תרבותי, הגינות חברתית
ומעורבות אזרחית.

החזון החינוכי מיושם בחיי בית-הספר
בחמישה מישורים מקבילים:

במישור האישי

מתן מענה לצרכים המגוונים של כלל התלמידים, תוך הכרת השונות ביניהם.

עידוד תלמידים למיצוי יכולותיהם. טיפוח מצוינות לימודית לצד סיוע לימודי

למתקשים

מגוון מסלולי לימוד

 

במישור הבית-ספרי והשכבתי 

דיאלוג מתמיד תוך כבוד הדדי בין תלמידים לבין הצוות החינוכי.

"מנהיגות רמון"​, יוזמות חינוכיות, חדר רמון, עמיתים, רדיו רמון.

במישור הקהילתי-יישובי

עידוד תלמידים להתנדב, לתרום ולהיות פעילים בקהילה:

התפתחות אישית ומעורבות חברתית, תעודת בגרות חברתית, יוזמות ב'פרויקט קבוצתי'​, התרמות.

במישור האזרחי-לאומי

הפעלת יוזמות חינוכיות להעמקת הזהות יהודית דמוקרטית, לצד טיפוח תודעה יהודית, הכרת ההיסטוריה והתרבות של עם ישראל, א"י ומדינת ישראל

תכנית פדגוגית לצד תכנית חברתית–ערכית.

במישור האוניברסלי

הבנת המשמעויות של היותנו גם "אזרחי העולם"​, בעיקר במציאות הגלובלית והאינטרנטית.

מסע לפולין, חילופי משלחות נוער, מיכנדורף-גרמניה, משלחת לאיטליה, קשרי תרבות וחינוך ישראל-סין.

companyResolutionResult.description:
Every company has a mission. What's ours? To empower every person and every organization to achieve more. We believe technology can and should be a force for good and that meaningful innovation contributes to a brighter world in the future and today. Our culture doesn’t just encourage curiosity; it embraces it. Each day we make progress together by showing up as our authentic selves. We show up with a learn-it-all mentality. We show up cheering on others, knowing their success doesn't diminish our own. We show up every day open to learning our own biases, changing our behavior, and inviting in differences. Because impact matters. 

Microsoft operates in 190 countries and is made up of approximately 228,000 passionate employees worldwide.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

companyResolutionResult.description:
Ceva is the leader in innovative silicon and software IP solutions
that enable smart edge products to connect, sense, and infer data more reliably and efficiently.
At Ceva, we are passionate about the smart edge. Providing the technology and market expertise our customers need to be successful is what we do best, and we’ve been doing it for over 30 years.

With the industry’s only portfolio of comprehensive communications and scalable edge AI IP, Ceva powers the connectivity, sensing, and inference in today’s most advanced smart edge products across consumer IoT, mobile, automotive, infrastructure, industrial, and personal computing. More than 18 billion of the world’s most innovative smart edge products from smartphones to drones to cellular base stations and more are powered by Ceva.

We create innovative technologies that help our customers turn great ideas into extraordinary products. We license our portfolio of wireless communications and scalable edge AI IP to our customers, breaking down barriers to entry and enabling them to bring new cutting-edge products to market faster, more reliably, efficiently, and economically.

Headquartered in Rockville, Maryland, Ceva has more than 400 employees worldwide, with design centers in Israel, Ireland, France, United Kingdom, United States and sales and support offices located in Europe, the U.S. and throughout Asia. To date, more than 17 billion CEVA-powered chips have been shipped worldwide, for a wide range of diverse end markets.

Ceva was created through the combination of the DSP IP licensing division of DSP Group, Inc. and Parthus Technologies plc (“Parthus”) in November 2002.
companyResolutionResult.name: Ceva, Inc.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Laurel Springs School is a fully accredited online private school for K-12 students worldwide. With 30+ years of experience in virtual learning, we’ve graduated over 6,200 students.

Our curriculum is self-paced and accessible 24/7 from anywhere in the world. This is why international and military families, entrepreneurs, athletes, and performing artists choose us! Our course catalog features engaging elementary courses that encourage creativity and involvement, 240+ college-prep courses (including Honors, AP®, and AP® Capstone) and 160+ NCAA-approved courses.

Students can join 30+ virtual clubs, attend in-person field trips and meetups, take on leadership roles, and participate in on-site prom and graduation.

Full-time students also build 1:1 relationships with their teachers, academic advisor, and counselor. This personalized support is one thing that sets our programs apart from other online schools.

Attend a Virtual Open House to learn more!
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Laurel Springs School

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Through a rigorous and dynamic American international education, WBAIS, a private secular school in Israel, inspires each student to cultivate a respect for diversity, develop a passion for life-long learning, achieve academic potential, assume leadership, contribute actively to society, and resolve conflict through dialogue and understanding.

Established under the auspices of the American Embassy over 50 years ago, WBAIS is a private, co-educational K-12 international school offering an American program leading to a US high school diploma. The school is structured in four levels: Preschool, Elementary School (K-5), Middle School (6-8), and High School (9-12). WBAIS is accredited by the Middle States Association of Colleges and Schools (MSA) and holds the MSA International Credential. The new state-of-the-art 19-acre campus is located in the town of Even Yehuda, a residential community approximately 14 miles north of Tel Aviv. The campus includes four separate schools, a performing arts building and auditorium, a gym, a full cafeteria, a library and media center, athletic fields, tennis and basketball courts, a pool, and many other facilities (including a dark room, art rooms, a fitness center, and a video station). The Jerusalem campus, JAIS, serves students from preschool through grade 12, and is a branch of WBAIS.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Walworth Barbour American International School in Israel

educations.schoolName: The Walworth Barbour American International School in Israel

schoolResolutionResult.name: The Walworth Barbour American International School in Israel

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description: Brookline High School reflects the high value the town holds for education having established a tradition of excellence and innovation, which has earned a national reputation. Serving nearly 2200 students, Brookline High represents the whole world, and it is a laboratory for the future of our global society.  The student body is diverse in terms of race, nationality, culture, and socio-economic strata.  The school is 9% African American, 14% Asian, 11% Hispanic, 61% White, and 5% Multi-Ethnic.  Students come from over 69 countries and speak 44 different languages.  We are proud of our diversity; and we have simultaneously created unity amidst the diversity, as our students pursue two common goals – academic success and exemplary citizenship.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Brookline High School

companyResolutionResult.description:
KayHut is looking for people who dare to do the UNBELIEVABLE on the daily basis.
We are a young, innovative, fast growing company engaged in cyber research and security solutions.
Currently we count over 100 employees and we are rapidly growing.
Our clients are the most demanding and sophisticated organizations in the government and private sectors.

We are looking for individuals with creative minds and exceptional skills in one or more of these areas:
·Reverse engineering
·Cyber-intelligence system engineering
·Vulnerability research
·Static and dynamic code analysis and algorithms
·OS kernel and internals

Our culture is known as FFF: a combination of Fun, Freedom and Fearlessness.

If this appeals to you and you’re ready to do the UNBELIEVABLE, talk to us. We’re eager to hear from you and have you join our team.

KayHut is unlike any other company you’ve ever worked for. We do what nobody else does and we have great fun doing it. Think this is UNBELIEVABLE? Let us surprise you!

Go to http://www.kayhut.com/#jobs to check out our latest job openings
companyResolutionResult.name: KayHut

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
“Shenkar - Engineering. Design. Art” has established itself as one of the leading colleges in Israel. Every year, Shenkar's graduates find themselves in the forefront of research and the industry, exhibit their works, participate in competitions and win prestigious awards.

Combining faculties of engineering, design and art is the basis of this institution’s unique multidisciplinary academics, allowing students and professors to combine cutting-edge technologies with contemporary design and artistic concepts. Each faculty has its own character, yet maintains fruitful collaborations with the other faculties, exposing students to a variety of disciplines and different fields of knowledge.

In the Pernick Faculty of Engineering, 5 departments offer a Bachelor’s Degree in Science (B.Sc.): the Department of Industrial Engineering and Management, the Department of Plastics Engineering, the Department of Software Engineering and the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering. A Master’s Degree program is offered in the Department of Plastics Engineering and the Department of Industrial Engineering and Management, which offers a unique program for those qualified.
In the Azrieli Faculty of Design, 6 departments offer a Bachelor’s Degree in Design (B.Des): The Department of Visual Communications, The Department of Textile Design, the Department of Interior - Building and Environment Design, the Department of Jewelry Design, the Department of Industrial Design and the Department of Fashion Design, considered one of the best of its kind worldwide.

In 2008, a new department was established, the Department of Multidisciplinary Art, which offers a Bachelor’s Degree in Fine Arts (B.F.A), and later, the unique program for a Master’s Degree in Design was introduced (M.Des), offering either a thesis or non-thesis routes for a degree in Fashion, Textile, Jewelry and Accessories Design.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Shenkar - Engineering. Design. Art.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ono Academic College was established in 1995 as a private, non-profit academic institution and has set itself two principal goals:

A. Developing and studying applied academic programs, which will enable graduates to integrate directly into the labor market, while maintaining the highest level of academic standards and teaching quality.

B. Giving top priority to the social problems of the State of Israel, by finding solutions for various sectors of the population, providing the opportunity for higher academic studies and, as a result, the ability to play an active role in the Israeli workforce.

Within this framework, we have developed study courses for outstanding students, enabling them to fulfill their potential and devote their time to multidisciplinary studies in more than one faculty, according to their choice.
In addition, Ono is proud to be the only higher education institute in Israel to include four academic hours of compulsory community work. We regard this personal commitment as an educational message of the highest level, which will result in the students' appreciation of Ono Academic College's worldview. Developing social sensitivity and awareness of the needs of one's community is an integral part of training future lawyers, accountants, and business professionals.

Ono Academic College is home to over 23,000 active students currently enrolled in various programs, including the undergraduate program in Law (LLB) and the undergraduate and graduate programs in Business Administration (BA and MBA) with a variety of specializations: Marketing and Advertising, Finance, Information Systems Analysis, Accounting, and Human Resources Management.

To date, over 60,000 graduates have successfully completed their studies at Ono Academic College and have gone on to make a significant impact in their respective fields.

We wish you success in your application for acceptance and hope to see you among us in the coming academic year.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ono Academic College

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

companyResolutionResult.description: OX Security removes risk at the source - eliminating exposures from AI code generation through cloud runtime - preventing security debt before it ever reaches production. organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Basmach - IDF School of Computer Science

ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
